name: Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support field change check for June 10, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support since the 2026-05-31 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran the first Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support field change check from the 2026-05-31T15:44:20.234Z public baseline publication through June 10, 2026. The three known arimoclomol registry-linked studies remain completed with posted results, and broad ClinicalTrials.gov searches found no same-window arimoclomol, Miplyffa, BGP-15, heat-shock-response, HSP70, HSF1, or passive-heating registry result that changes the public outlook. PubMed returned two post-baseline arimoclomol or Miplyffa-indexed records: an NPC therapeutics review and a 2024 FDA approvals drug-interaction review. Neither adds new primary efficacy evidence or changes the current NPC-specific interpretation. A same-window Zevra Orange Book patent-listing announcement is context-only intellectual-property activity. No public source, study, finding, activity item, outlook, or candidate bundle is warranted. The pass did identify a material pre-baseline coverage gap: PMID 40663813, a 48-month open-label extension of the NPC-002 arimoclomol trial, plus related label, drug-interaction, registry-watch, and taxonomy-boundary coverage. A coverage assessment was created and recommends coverage repair before ordinary surveillance resumes.

materiality_decision.rationale: No same-window source changes the public stage, momentum, read firmness, or support map. The material issue is internal source-completeness risk: the current public baseline does not represent long-term NPC open-label extension evidence, fuller Miplyffa label or drug-interaction context, or the broader arimoclomol/BGP-15/heat-shock registry boundary set. Those should be handled through a bounded coverage-repair pass rather than an ad hoc surveillance bundle.

search_log.summary: The audit found three registry-linked interventional studies for the track. All three have trial_details and are locally marked completed with posted results, but all three had stale or missing local registry check dates and no local completion dates.

search_log.query: Known registry re-checks: NCT02612129, NCT03491462, and NCT02753530
search_log.summary: NCT02612129, NCT03491462, and NCT02753530 remain completed with results. NCT02612129 lists actual primary completion on 2018-06-25 and actual study completion on 2024-10-31; the ALS and IBM trials list actual completion in 2020 and 2021. No same-window status or results change was found.

Returned the known NPC, ALS, and IBM trials plus older or adjacent arimoclomol records including ALS open-label extension, IBM open-label extension, Gaucher disease, early access NPC, old SOD1 ALS, and withdrawn or dose-ranging ALS records. These were pre-baseline, wrong-scope, no-results, or already represented by the published ALS/IBM/NPC evidence.

Returned NCT01069965, a terminated type 2 diabetes BGP-15 dose-ranging study with no posted results and no same-window update. It is pre-baseline biomarker or metabolic boundary context, not a public delta.

search_log.query: "heat shock response" aging; "passive heating" aging HSP70; "heat shock protein" OR HSP70 OR HSF1
search_log.summary: Returned passive-heating aging physiology records, heatwave and thermoregulation studies, an HSP90 PET imaging study, HSP90 oncology records, and other wrong-scope or no-results records. No result-bearing chaperone or heat-shock-support intervention record changed the public outlook.

search_log.query: (arimoclomol OR Miplyffa) with publication dates 2026-05-31 through 2026-06-10
search_log.summary: Returned PMID 42007957, an NPC therapeutics review that repeats arimoclomol approval and phase II/III context, and PMID 41567854, a review of drug-interaction data from 2024 FDA approvals that includes arimoclomol transporter-inhibitor context. Neither is a new primary efficacy or clinical-outcome result.

search_log.query: BGP-15, BGP15, heat shock response, HSP70, HSF1, heat shock protein, chaperone, proteostasis, aging, lifespan, longevity, and intervention terms with publication dates 2026-05-31 through 2026-06-10
search_log.summary: BGP-15 terms returned zero same-window records. Broad heat-shock and chaperone searches returned mechanistic, plant, forensic, cancer, infection, proteasome, mitochondrial-proteostasis, and disease-model records. No record was a same-window human chaperone-support efficacy, target-engagement, safety, correction, or retraction result for the scoped track.

search_log.source_name: PubMed and web lead screen for pre-baseline coverage gaps
search_log.query: arimoclomol open-label extension 48-month NPC, arimoclomol first approval, Miplyffa label, Miplyffa real-world data, and arimoclomol drug interaction context
search_log.summary: Found pre-baseline but important coverage-repair leads: PMID 40663813 reports 48-month open-label extension efficacy and safety for NPC-002; DailyMed lists a Miplyffa label updated March 18, 2026; sponsor and conference materials describe pre-baseline real-world/post-approval Miplyffa context; and PMID 41567854 summarizes drug-interaction data from 2024 approvals.

search_log.source_name: FDA, DailyMed, and sponsor web searches
search_log.query: Miplyffa arimoclomol label update, FDA approval, Zevra Miplyffa June 2026, and Orange Book patent listing
search_log.summary: The FDA approval remains the public regulatory anchor. DailyMed showed a March 18, 2026 label update predating the baseline. Zevra announced on June 8, 2026 that it submitted a Miplyffa patent for Orange Book listing; that is same-window IP activity, not clinical or regulatory evidence that changes the public outlook.

trial_watch_checks.summary: The NPC-002 registry remains completed with posted results. The registry now supplies actual completion dates absent from the local study record, and PubMed source discovery found a 48-month OLE paper that should be reviewed during coverage repair.

The ORARIALS-01 ALS registry remains completed with posted results and no same-window interpretation change. The completion dates were missing locally but do not change the public null-trial boundary.

The inclusion body myositis registry remains completed with posted results and no same-window interpretation change. The completion dates were missing locally but do not change the public null-trial boundary.

excluded_sources.title: Long-term efficacy and safety of arimoclomol in Niemann-Pick disease type C: Final results of the phase 2/3 NPC-002 48-month open-label extension trial.
excluded_sources.reason: Directly relevant arimoclomol NPC durability and safety evidence, but it was published before the May 31 baseline. It should be handled through coverage repair with source-fidelity and interpretation review rather than silently inserted during same-window surveillance.

excluded_sources.title: Current and emerging therapeutic advancements for Niemann-Pick disease type C: where are we now and what does the future hold?
excluded_sources.reason: Post-baseline PubMed publication date but review-level NPC context, with no new primary arimoclomol efficacy or safety result beyond the already represented approval and trial context.

excluded_sources.title: Understanding Pharmacokinetic-Drug Interactions With Drugs Approved by the US Food and Drug Administration in 2024 to Better Manage the Risk of Drug Interactions With Concomitant Medications.
excluded_sources.reason: Drug-interaction review includes arimoclomol transporter-inhibitor context, but it does not change the evidence stage or same-window public interpretation. It belongs in a safety-focused coverage repair if selected.

excluded_sources.reference: Zevra June 8, 2026 Miplyffa Orange Book patent-listing announcement
excluded_sources.title: Zevra Therapeutics Announces Orange Book Listing of U.S. Patent Covering MIPLYFFA.
excluded_sources.reason: Same-window external activity, but it is intellectual-property context rather than evidence, safety, trial, regulatory-approval, or public-outlook movement.

excluded_sources.title: Arimoclomol in Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis - Open Label Extension Trial
excluded_sources.reason: Pre-baseline terminated ALS open-label extension registry with posted results. It may be useful during coverage repair for safety or durability boundaries, but it is not a same-window delta.

excluded_sources.title: Arimoclomol in Sporadic Inclusion Body Myositis - Open Label Extension Trial
excluded_sources.reason: Pre-baseline terminated IBM open-label extension registry with posted results. The public baseline already captures the parent IBM trial's null functional result; extension details require coverage repair if selected.

excluded_sources.title: Study of Arimoclomol in Patients Diagnosed With Gaucher Disease Type 1 or 3
excluded_sources.reason: Terminated Gaucher disease arimoclomol registry without posted results. It is disease-specific lysosomal context, not broad aging or heat-shock-support evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Early Access Program With Arimoclomol in US Patients With NPC
excluded_sources.reason: Available early-access NPC program with no posted results. It may matter for post-approval context but does not change evidence interpretation during this surveillance window.

excluded_sources.title: Safety and Efficacy of BGP-15 in Patients With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
excluded_sources.reason: Terminated BGP-15 metabolic disease registry with no posted results and no same-window update. It may be reviewed as BGP-15 boundary context during coverage repair.

excluded_sources.title: Heat, Microvascular Function and Aging
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting passive-heating aging physiology registry with no posted results. It does not establish chaperone or heat-shock target engagement or functional aging benefit.

excluded_sources.title: Chronic Passive Heating in Healthy Older Adults
excluded_sources.reason: Completed passive-heating older-adult registry with no posted results. It is heat physiology context, not a same-window chaperone-support update.

excluded_sources.title: Acute Passive Heating in Healthy Older Adults
excluded_sources.reason: Completed passive-heating older-adult registry with no posted results. It is heat physiology context, not a same-window chaperone-support update.

excluded_sources.title: Quantification of Hsp90 in the Human Brain
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting PET imaging study in Parkinson disease, Alzheimer disease, and ALS. It measures Hsp90 biology but is not a chaperone-support intervention result.

excluded_sources.title: Oxidative Stress and Diminished Mitochondrial Proteostatic Reserve Are Linked to Enhanced mtUPR Initiation in Aged Mouse Muscle.
excluded_sources.reason: Same-window mammalian aging mechanistic source around mitochondrial proteostatic reserve and mtUPR initiation, but it is not a chaperone-support intervention or human outcome result.

notes: Triage selected this as the rank-1 ready surveillance item for the loss_of_proteostasis hallmark.
The delta window was anchored to publication event publish-chaperone-and-heat-shock-support-bootstrap-2026-05-31-2026-05-31t15-44-20-234z.
This track had no prior coverage assessment. The new assessment remains thin and recommends coverage repair before ordinary surveillance resumes.
PMID 40663813 is pre-baseline but materially relevant to safety and durability framing; it should be reviewed in coverage repair rather than treated as a same-window public delta.
The same-window Zevra patent listing does not belong in public activity because it is IP context and does not alter evidence interpretation.
next_actions: Run bounded coverage repair focused on the arimoclomol NPC 48-month open-label extension, label and drug-interaction context, and the broader arimoclomol/BGP-15/heat-exposure registry boundary set.
During repair, decide whether PMID 40663813 should become a staged source, study update, finding, and outlook support-map adjustment for NPC-specific durability and safety.
Review whether Miplyffa label, DailyMed, drug-interaction review, and sponsor real-world/conference materials belong as public source context, explicit exclusions, or internal notes.

Create a public bundle only after source-fidelity and interpretation review show that missing sources materially improve the evidence map while preserving the current NPC-specific and tentative broad-aging boundary.
